data.title: Support file exclusion patterns in enforce command

data.description: Allow skipping files by glob pattern (e.g. --exclude README.md,index.md) or via an ignore list in schema.toml. Also consider adding frontmatter to files that have none.

data.title: Warn on stderr when skipping unreadable files

data.description: query.rs and sr.rs silently skip files with Err(_) => continue. Should emit eprintln warning so user knows files were excluded from results.

data.title: Fix max_depth(1) vs recursive help text mismatch

data.description: CLI help says 'walks folder recursively' but collect_md_files uses max_depth(1). Either enable recursion or fix docs.

data.title: Add default date to daily-discipline schema

data.description: stats.md and template.md fail enforce because date has no schema default. Consider adding a sensible default or making date optional for non-daily files.

data.title: Phase out YAML reader once vault fully migrated

data.description: Per migration plan step 5: once all vault files use TOML (+++) frontmatter, remove YAML fallback path and serde_yaml dependency.
Set up cross-compilation for aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu target. Add cargo cross or rustup target, produce release binary for aarch64 (e.g. ARM servers, Apple Silicon via Linux).

data.title: Epic: clap_describe crate + cargo workspace conversion

data.description: Create a reusable clap_describe crate that adds --describe flag to clap subcommands. Outputs structured CommandSchema (JSON via --json, markdown otherwise) describing command purpose, args, types, defaults, possible values. Designed for agent/LLM consumption. Convert md-fme to cargo workspace to house both crates.

Convert the single-crate project into a cargo workspace with two members: md-fme (the CLI binary) and clap_describe (the library). Move Cargo.toml to workspace root, create crates/md-fme/ and crates/clap_describe/ directories. Ensure existing tests still pass.

Define the core data model: CommandSchema, ArgSchema, SubcommandSummary structs with Serialize derive. CommandSchema holds name, about, long_about, usage, args (positional + options), subcommands list, and optional extra_description text. ArgSchema holds name, short, long, required, default_value, possible_values, help, value_name, takes_value.

Implement extract_schema(&clap::Command) -> CommandSchema function. Walks the Command tree using get_about, get_long_about, get_arguments, get_positionals, get_subcommands. Extracts arg metadata: short/long flags, required, defaults, possible values, action type. Also provide extract_schema_with_extra for custom additional text.

Implement to_markdown() method on CommandSchema. Outputs a clean markdown description of the command: name, purpose, usage line, arguments table (name, type, required, default, description), subcommands list. Designed for human + LLM readability.

Provide Describe struct deriving clap::Args with a --describe bool flag. Users flatten this into subcommand structs. Also provide a helper function handle_describe(describe: &Describe, cmd: &Command, json: bool) -> Option<String> that returns Some(output) if --describe was set, None otherwise. The json param controls whether to serialize CommandSchema as JSON or call to_markdown().

data.title: md-fme: Integrate clap_describe into all subcommands

data.description: Add #[command(flatten)] describe: clap_describe::Describe to every subcommand variant in Commands enum. In main.rs match arms, check describe flag first and short-circuit with describe output before normal execution. Pass cli.json to control output format.

data.title: Integration tests for --describe on all subcommands

data.description: Add integration tests that verify: (1) each subcommand responds to --describe with markdown output containing command name, args, usage, (2) each subcommand responds to --describe --json with valid JSON matching CommandSchema structure, (3) --describe short-circuits and does NOT execute the command, (4) --describe works alongside --json global flag.

data.title: clap_describe: make --describe work without required args

data.description: Clap validates required args before our code runs, so subcommands with required args (query, today, stats, review) need dummy values alongside --describe. Research options: custom parser, making --describe override arg requirements, or using clap's mut_subcommand to mark args optional when --describe is present.

data.title: Research: terminal markdown/table rendering crates for --describe output

data.description: Current --describe markdown output renders raw markdown tables which look bad in terminal. Research Rust crates for: (1) rendering markdown tables as formatted ASCII tables in terminal (comfy-table, tabled, prettytable-rs, term-table), (2) full terminal markdown rendering (termimad, mdcat, pulldown-cmark + custom renderer). Also research how googleworkspace/cli (https://github.com/googleworkspace/cli) implements their describe command for inspiration. Goal: --describe outputs nicely formatted ASCII tables instead of raw markdown pipe tables.
